title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode VII.
content:
The women tell me every day
    That all my bloom has pas past away.
    "Behold," the pretty wantons cry,
    "Behold this mirror with a sigh;
    The locks upon thy brow are few,
    And like the rest, they're withering too!"
    Whether decline has thinned my hair,
    I'm sure I neither know nor care;
    But this I know, and this I feel
    As onward to the tomb I steal,
    That still as death approaches nearer,
    The joys of life are sweeter, dearer;
    And had I but an hour to live,
    That little hour to bliss I'd give.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode VII.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
